specification.topic-statement: The LLM message submission system sends a user message to the provider and manages the full request lifecycle from input validation through async stream initiation.

scope.in-scope: Handling the Enter key action (routing to slash commands or LLM submission), creating and persisting the user message, clearing prior response state, transitioning agent state, building the normalized provider request, and initiating the async HTTP stream.
scope.boundary: If the input begins with '/', it is forwarded to the command dispatcher. Command execution behavior is handled externally.
The normalized request is handed off to the provider's streaming interface. Streaming events and completion callbacks are registered but the provider manages all HTTP communication.
User messages and agent idle status are persisted via database helpers. Persistence errors are logged but do not block the request.
Stream event handling and response completion processing are handled by the LLM stream callback system, registered here but executed externally.

field.name: first character
field.note: '/' triggers slash command path; anything else is user message

field.name: data_json
field.note: object with model, temperature, max_completion_tokens from config

behavior.enter-key-dispatch:
The text and length of the current input buffer are read.
Any active completion is dismissed regardless of input content.
If the first character of the input is '/': the input buffer is cleared and the viewport offset is reset to zero before any command processing. A null-terminated copy of the input text is made; if allocation fails, the process aborts. The copy is forwarded to the command dispatcher. If the command is the legacy pretty-print command ("/pp"), it is handled locally by formatting and appending the input buffer state to the scrollback. All other slash commands are forwarded to the general command dispatcher; any error returned is logged and freed.
If the first character is not '/': the current input text is appended to the scrollback (making the user's message visible in the conversation history). If the text is non-empty and global configuration is present, a null-terminated copy is made and sent to the provider. If the text is empty, no action is taken. The copy is freed after sending.
Returns success unconditionally.

sending-to-provider.model-validation: If the agent's model field is null or empty, an error message is appended to the scrollback and the operation returns immediately without sending.
sending-to-provider.user-message-creation: A user-role text message is created and added to the agent's in-memory conversation array.
sending-to-provider.token-cache-turn: If a token cache is present on the agent, a new turn boundary is recorded (incrementing the turn counter and initializing the new turn as uncached).
sending-to-provider.database-persistence: If a database context and valid session ID are present, the user message is persisted. A JSON metadata object containing the model, temperature, and max completion tokens from configuration is constructed and included. If the database insert fails, a warning is logged via JSONL and the operation continues.
sending-to-provider.response-buffer-reset: The agent's previous assistant response string and streaming line buffer are freed if they exist and set to null. The tool iteration counter is reset to zero.
sending-to-provider.active-status-update: If a database context is present, the agent's idle flag is set to false in the database. Errors are logged and ignored.
sending-to-provider.state-transition: The agent transitions from IDLE to WAITING_FOR_LLM. This makes the spinner visible and hides the input buffer.
sending-to-provider.provider-initialization: The agent's provider instance is retrieved (lazily created if not cached). If provider creation fails, the error message is appended to the scrollback and the agent transitions back to IDLE.
sending-to-provider.request-building: A normalized provider request is built from the conversation: the agent's model, system prompt, message history, tool definitions (filtered by the agent's toolset filter if present), and thinking level. If request building fails, the error is appended to the scrollback and the agent transitions back to IDLE.
sending-to-provider.stream-initiation: The provider's streaming interface is called with the request and two callbacks: a stream event callback for incremental updates and a completion callback for final response processing, both receiving the agent as context. If stream initiation fails, the error is appended to the scrollback and the agent transitions back to IDLE. On success, the agent's HTTP-in-flight flag is set.
